title: Trade-in BackBox - How does it work?
bodyHtml:
While you're already familiar with our Sales BackBox, we're rolling out a revamped Trade-in BackBox as of June 2025.

 

Similar to sales, getting the Trade-in BackBox is no longer only about proposing the best price. The goal of the Trade-in BackBox is to give the final customer an offer at the best price with reduced counteroffer risk while rewarding refurbishers with stronger circularity on Back Market.

 

How is the Trade-in Backbox computed?

The Trade-in BackBox algorithm now integrates 3 metrics to determine your standing in the BackBox:

 

  1. Your price offer
  2. Your counteroffer rate: This key quality metric measures the percentage of orders for which you made a counteroffer. It can be viewed in your Back Office. Read all about the counteroffer rate here.
  3. Your circularity rate: This key performance metric measures how many Trade-in products you've bought and then sold on Back Market over the past 6 weeks. It is computed at the device level. It will be available in your Back Office shortly (Sales > Insights > Trade-in). Read all about the circularity rate here.


In short, the counteroffer rate and the circularity rate are now essential to winning the BackBox for you at the most competitive – lowest –  price.

 

What about your ability to set your prices?

On the Listings page of your Trade-in Back Office, you can set and change your prices. You will see: 

 

  • Your price: This is the current price you have defined for a specific product.
  • Price to win BackBox: This is the price you need to set to win the Trade-in BackBox. It can be lower or higher than the current BackBox price depending on your counteroffer rate and circularity rate.
  • BackBox price: This is the price currently offered to customers on the platform. It belongs to the seller with the ideal combination of price, counteroffer rate, and circularity rate. 

 

 

Similarly, if you are updating your prices through CSV or with the API, you will access the “price_to_win” information to be able to set your prices easily at your “Price to Win BackBox”, i.e. to easily get BackBoxes.

 

Good to know Q&A

Where can I find the price to win the BackBox? How do I know the prices of competitors?

For each product in each grade and each market, the Price to win the BackBox will always be displayed in your Back Office > Listings (see above screenshot) and on the CSV export of your listings (price_to_win).

You will not be able to know the price of competitors. But, you will be able to know the current BackBox price and see how your quality performance impacts your price to win.

 

If I win the Trade-in BackBox, will customers be offered my price?

Yes. If you win the BackBox, the price that will be displayed to the customer will be your price. Trade-ins for this product will be directed to you. 

 

My counteroffer rate or my circularity rate doesn’t seem to be good enough, I am not able to get any Trade-in BackBox, but I don't know what to do.

Don’t stress! Reach out to your Back Market contact and explain the situation. Together you will work on an adapted action plan to help you improve your counteroffer rate and/or resell more devices on Back Market.

 

[Circularity] What do I do if there is not enough demand for a device to sell it back on Back Market?

Your circularity rate is assessed compared to the average circularity rate of all sellers on the device you are willing to source. So in that case, low demand for a device will also impact other sellers in the same proportion. Read all about circularity rate here.

 

[Counteroffer rate] I’m doing low volumes. This can make my counteroffer rate highly volatile.

No worries, for you to not be impacted by the high volatility of your counteroffer rate when you have too low volumes (e.g. only 3 orders received in your last 30 days, and 67% CO rate with only 2 counteroffers), by default all refurbishers have 10 extra orders received added to the computation with the average CO rate of Back Market.
